passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: Hardly had the government announced the new tax regulations when several major corporations threatened to move their headquarters abroad.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text:
Professor: The midterm results were quite disappointing for most of the class.
Student: Do you think the questions were too difficult, or were we just unprepared?
Professor: ----
Student: That makes sense. I noticed many people left the analytical sections blank.

questions.question: The newly discovered enzyme acts as a crucial ---- in the metabolic process, significantly accelerating the breakdown of complex carbohydrates into glucose.

options.A: catalyst
options.B: deterrent
options.C: obstacle
options.D: liability
options.E: precedent

questions.question: Despite the rigorous safety protocols, the chemical plant remains ---- to potential leaks due to aging infrastructure.

options.A: immune
options.B: vulnerable
options.C: resistant
options.D: indifferent
options.E: impervious

questions.question: Recent archeological findings in Mesopotamia have ---- traditional theories about the timeline of early urban development.

options.A: corroborated
options.B: undermined
options.C: reconciled
options.D: perpetuated
options.E: facilitated

questions.question: Although many experts argued that the economic stimulus would lead to inflation, ----.

options.A: the central bank decided to raise interest rates immediately
options.B: it actually resulted in a period of unprecedented price stability
options.C: most consumers began to spend their savings rapidly
options.D: the government was forced to reduce its budget deficit
options.E: global markets reacted with high levels of volatility

questions.question: Unless the international community takes decisive action to reduce carbon emissions, ----.

options.A: renewable energy sources will become more expensive
options.B: the targets set by the Paris Agreement will remain attainable
options.C: the frequency of extreme weather events is expected to escalate
options.D: deforestation in the Amazon has been partially reversed
options.E: industrialized nations have already met their obligations

questions.question: ---- that the human brain continues to develop and rewire itself well into early adulthood.

options.A: It is now widely accepted by neuroscientists
options.B: The hypothesis was rejected decades ago
options.C: Many people still believe mistakenly
options.D: Some researchers are hesitant to claim
options.E: Hardly anyone would dare to suggest

questions.question: Since the local government failed to allocate sufficient funds for urban renewal, ----.

options.A: the city has become a hub for tech startups
options.B: public transportation systems have undergone a massive overhaul
options.C: the historical district has fallen into a state of disrepair
options.D: more parks and recreational areas are being constructed
options.E: tourism revenue has reached an all-time high

questions.question: Cloze Test (Part 1): The industrial revolution was a period of (1) ---- change that transformed agricultural societies into industrial ones. (1)

options.A: minimal
options.B: profound
options.C: superficial
options.D: temporary
options.E: gradual

questions.question: Cloze Test (Part 2): This era saw the introduction of machinery (2) ---- replaced manual labor. (2)

options.A: where
options.B: whose
options.C: whom
options.D: which
options.E: when

questions.question: Cloze Test (Part 3): (3) ---- the increase in production, many workers faced harsh conditions. (3)

options.A: Despite
options.B: Because
options.C: Instead of
options.D: In addition to
options.E: Unlike

questions.question: Cloze Test (Part 4): Urban populations grew (4) ---- as people moved to cities for work. (4)

options.A: exponentially
options.B: scarcely
options.C: vaguely
options.D: reluctantly
options.E: incidentally

questions.question: Cloze Test (Part 5): Eventually, these shifts led to the (5) ---- of modern labor laws. (5)

options.A: abolition
options.B: establishment
options.C: rejection
options.D: neglect
options.E: suspension

questions.question: Kuantum fiziĐi, atom altı paracıkların davranışını inceleyen ve modern teknolojinin temelini oluşturan bir bilim dalıdır.

options.A: Quantum physics is a branch of science that examines the behavior of subatomic particles and forms the basis of modern technology.
options.B: Modern technology is based on quantum physics, which studies the movement of atoms.
options.C: The study of subatomic particles in quantum physics has led to the development of modern technology.
options.D: Quantum physics, which is essential for technology, focuses on how particles behave.
options.E: Modern technology relies on the science of quantum physics to understand atoms.

questions.question: Despite the high cost of implementation, solar energy remains one of the most promising alternatives to fossil fuels.

options.A: Fosil yakıtlara en iyi alternatif g�neş enerjisi olsa da uygulama maliyeti y�ksektir.
options.B: Uygulama maliyetinin y�ksek olmasına rağmen, g�neş enerjisi fosil yakıtlara en vaat edici alternatiflerden biri olmayı s�rd�rmektedir.
options.C: G�neş enerjisi maliyetli bir y�ntemdir ancak fosil yakıtların yerini alabilir.
options.D: Fosil yakıtların yerine ge�ebilecek en ucuz alternatif g�neş enerjisidir.
options.E: G�neş enerjisi y�ksek maliyetine rağmen fosil yakıtlardan daha fazla tercih edilmektedir.

questions.question: Eğitim sistemindeki reformların temel amacı, �ğrencilerin eleştirel d�ş�nme becerilerini geliştirmektir.

options.A: The main goal of the reforms in the education system is to develop students' critical thinking skills.
options.B: Critical thinking skills are being developed through reforms in the education system.
options.C: Students need critical thinking skills, which is the aim of the educational reform.
options.D: Reforming the education system will help students think more critically.
options.E: To improve critical thinking, the education system must be reformed immediately.

questions.question: The rise of social media has fundamentally altered the way information is disseminated and consumed worldwide.

options.A: Sosyal medyanın y�kselişinin temel sebebi bilginin t�m d�nyada hızlıca yayılmasıdır.
options.B: Bilgi t�m d�nyada sosyal medya aracılığıyla yayılmakta ve t�ketilmektedir.
options.C: Sosyal medyanın y�kselişi, bilginin d�nya �apında yayılma ve t�ketilme bi�imini temelden değıştirmiştir.
options.D: Sosyal medya sayesinde bilgi artık d�nya genelinde daha farklı bir şekilde t�ketiliyor.
options.E: Bilginin yayılmasındaki değışim sosyal medyanın y�kselişine bağlanabilir.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: As soon as the new tax rules were made public, many large companies warned that they might relocate to other countries.
options.B: Major corporations moved their headquarters because the government announced new tax regulations.
options.C: The government announced the tax regulations after the corporations had already moved abroad.
options.D: Because the government delayed the tax announcement, corporations stayed in the country.
options.E: Corporations threatened to move only before the government announced the tax regulations.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Scientists have always been concerned about the climate since the early 20th century.
options.B: Human activity began to affect the climate only in the late 20th century.
options.C: Only towards the end of the 20th century did scientists start to focus seriously on how humans affect the climate.
options.D: The global climate changed significantly in the late 20th century due to scientific research.
options.E: Human activity was not considered a problem by scientists before the 20th century ended.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The manager approved the project despite the unrealistic budget proposal.
options.B: The project was rejected because the budget proposal was not practical.
options.C: Even if the budget were realistic, the manager would not have approved the project.
options.D: The manager did not look at the budget proposal, so the project failed.
options.E: A realistic budget proposal was submitted, but the manager still rejected it.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: People believe the Great Wall is visible from the moon, and they are correct.
options.B: The Great Wall can be seen from space, but not from the moon.
options.C: Despite what most people think, you cannot see the Great Wall from the moon without equipment.
options.D: The Great Wall is the only man-made structure visible from the moon.
options.E: Because it is so large, many people think the Great Wall is visible from the moon.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I didn't know bees were that important for our survival.
options.B: So, their disappearance would have a direct impact on global food security.
options.C: Maybe we should start using artificial pollination methods instead.
options.D: Bees are not the only insects that help with pollination, though.
options.E: The report suggests that pesticides are the main cause of the decline.

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The questions were actually easier than last year's exam.
options.B: I believe the issue was more about a lack of critical analysis rather than factual knowledge.
options.C: I will consider curving the grades to help everyone pass.
options.D: You should have spent more time in the library studying the textbook.
options.E: Most students performed exceptionally well in the multiple-choice section.

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: That sounds promising. Can you provide specific examples of your leadership in those cases?
options.B: We prefer candidates who have managed teams of more than fifty people.
options.C: Leadership is something that cannot be learned; you either have it or you don't.
options.D: Thank you for coming in, we will let you know our decision next week.
options.E: Could you explain why you decided to leave your previous company?

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: You must stop eating out entirely if you want to get better.
options.B: Maybe you should try cooking at home more often and using herbs for flavor.
options.C: I will have to prescribe a stronger medication if this continues.
options.D: Salt is necessary for the body, but only in very small amounts.
options.E: High blood pressure is a common problem in people your age.

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Hackers are becoming increasingly sophisticated in their methods.
options.B: Many people still use simple passwords for their personal accounts.
options.C: Social media platforms are the primary targets for cybercriminals.
options.D: Computers are faster today than they were ten years ago.
options.E: Most software updates are designed to fix minor bugs.

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: They often worked outdoors to observe natural light directly.
options.B: Oil paints were the only medium used during this period.
options.C: Many Impressionist artists were born in the late 19th century.
options.D: The Louvre museum houses some of the most famous paintings.
options.E: Art critics in the 1800s were known for being very conservative.

questions.question: The concept of 'emotional intelligence' has gained widespread popularity in the workplace. It refers to the ability to recognize, understand, and manage one's own emotions as well as those of others. ----. Research shows that leaders with high emotional intelligence tend to have more motivated and productive teams.

options.A: Technical skills are often more important than social skills.
options.B: Many employees prefer working alone rather than in groups.
options.C: This skill is particularly crucial for effective leadership and conflict resolution.
options.D: Emotional intelligence is a trait that cannot be improved over time.
options.E: Most companies focus only on IQ when hiring new managers.

questions.question: Sustainable architecture aims to minimize the negative environmental impact of buildings. This is achieved through efficiency and moderation in the use of materials, energy, and development space. ----. For instance, using solar panels and rainwater harvesting systems can significantly reduce a building's carbon footprint.

options.A: Modern buildings are often made of glass and steel.
options.B: The integration of renewable energy sources is a key component of this approach.
options.C: Architects must follow local building codes and regulations.
options.D: Most people prefer living in traditional houses rather than modern ones.
options.E: Construction costs have risen sharply due to inflation.

questions.question: The evidence presented during the trial was so overwhelming that the jury reached a verdict in less than an hour.

options.A: The jury was very fast because the evidence was extremely convincing and clear.
options.B: The trial lasted less than an hour because there was no evidence.
options.C: The jury had a hard time deciding the verdict despite the evidence.
options.D: Because the evidence was weak, the jury reached a verdict quickly.
options.E: The evidence was presented so quickly that the jury was confused.

questions.question: Rarely do we encounter a scientific discovery that has the potential to change our understanding of the universe so fundamentally.

options.A: Scientific discoveries usually change our understanding of the universe.
options.B: It is very unusual to find a discovery that changes our view of the universe so much.
options.C: We often see discoveries that are fundamental to our understanding of science.
options.D: Most scientific discoveries are not understood by the general public.
options.E: The universe is so complex that no discovery can ever change our view of it.

questions.question: The company's decision to expand into the Asian market was prompted by a significant increase in demand for its products there.

options.A: The company expanded to Asia even though there was no demand.
options.B: The increase in demand in Asia led the company to decide to expand its operations there.
options.C: The company wanted to create demand in Asia by expanding its market.
options.D: Asian markets are difficult to enter despite high demand for products.
options.E: The company decided not to expand until demand increased in Asia.

questions.question: (I) The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic rebirth following the Middle Ages. (II) It took place from the 14th century to the 17th century, promoting the rediscovery of classical philosophy, literature and art. (III) Some of the greatest thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists and artists in human history thrived during this era. (IV) The Black Death, which occurred earlier, had killed nearly half of Europe's population. (V) Global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll. (II) This process generally involves the green pigment chlorophyll and generates oxygen as a byproduct. (III) Most plants are green because they contain a large amount of chlorophyll. (IV) Oxygen is essential for the survival of almost all living organisms on Earth. (V) Without photosynthesis, the atmosphere would lack the oxygen necessary for life.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Virtual Reality (VR) is a simulated experience that can be similar to or completely different from the real world. (II) Applications of virtual reality include entertainment, education, and business. (III) VR headsets are becoming more affordable for the average consumer. (IV) Augmented reality, on the other hand, overlays digital information onto the real world. (V) Many medical schools are now using VR to train surgeons in a risk-free environment.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system. It is home to thousands of species of marine life, including fish, whales, and turtles. However, the reef is facing a severe threat from climate change. Rising ocean temperatures cause coral bleaching, a process where corals expel the algae living in their tissues, turning them white and making them more susceptible to disease. Question: According to the passage, what is the primary cause of coral bleaching?

options.A: Overfishing in the Australian coast
options.B: The presence of too many marine species
options.C: Rising temperatures in the ocean
options.D: Pollution from nearby industrial plants
options.E: The natural aging process of the coral

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system. It is home to thousands of species of marine life, including fish, whales, and turtles. However, the reef is facing a severe threat from climate change. Rising ocean temperatures cause coral bleaching, a process where corals expel the algae living in their tissues, turning them white and making them more susceptible to disease. Question: What happens to corals during the bleaching process?

options.A: They grow faster to compensate for the heat.
options.B: They turn white and become more prone to illness.
options.C: They attract more algae to their tissues.
options.D: They provide more food for the fish and whales.
options.E: They become immune to any future temperature changes.

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system. It is home to thousands of species of marine life, including fish, whales, and turtles. However, the reef is facing a severe threat from climate change. Rising ocean temperatures cause coral bleaching, a process where corals expel the algae living in their tissues, turning them white and making them more susceptible to disease. Question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a resident of the reef?

options.A: Whales
options.B: Fish
options.C: Turtles
options.D: Sharks
options.E: Marine life

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked a major turning point in history. It shifted the economy from manual labor and draft-animal-based manufacturing to machine-based manufacturing. This transition was fueled by the invention of the steam engine, which allowed for the mass production of goods. While it led to economic growth, it also caused significant social changes, including urbanization and the rise of the working class. Question: What was the main factor that enabled mass production during the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: The use of draft animals in factories
options.B: The invention of the steam engine
options.C: The decrease in the urban population
options.D: The reliance on manual labor
options.E: The decline of the British economy

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked a major turning point in history. It shifted the economy from manual labor and draft-animal-based manufacturing to machine-based manufacturing. This transition was fueled by the invention of the steam engine, which allowed for the mass production of goods. While it led to economic growth, it also caused significant social changes, including urbanization and the rise of the working class. Question: According to the passage, the Industrial Revolution caused ----.

options.A: a decrease in the number of people living in cities
options.B: a shift back to agricultural-based economies
options.C: the rise of a new class of workers
options.D: the end of international trade
options.E: a slower production process for most goods

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked a major turning point in history. It shifted the economy from manual labor and draft-animal-based manufacturing to machine-based manufacturing. This transition was fueled by the invention of the steam engine, which allowed for the mass production of goods. While it led to economic growth, it also caused significant social changes, including urbanization and the rise of the working class. Question: The passage implies that before the late 18th century, ----.

options.A: manufacturing was mostly done by hand or animals
options.B: steam engines were already common in Britain
options.C: most people lived in large urban centers
options.D: the economy was based on machine production
options.E: Britain was not a global power
